fit_validation.note: Same-link shell overlap is expected because generated meshes are cosmetic outer surfaces. Adjacent kinematic interfaces are reported separately from non-adjacent production clearance. Non-adjacent body clearance and joint sweep checks are hard gates for production fit. The primary MJCF keeps bodykit geoms visual-only; the collision-test MJCF enables contacts for MuJoCo inspection but uses unreduced cosmetic meshes, not final collision proxies. Production release still needs CAD boolean checks against final R1 CAD/scan.

panel_gap_validation.note: This is sampled mesh-to-mesh evidence for nearby bodykit parts in the home pose. Face inserts use a seated-detail gate instead of the normal rigid panel seam gate, but they remain included in the mechanical stress clearance sweep. Production release still needs exact CAD gap checks after split lines, mounts, and inner shell offsets are surfaced.

feet_ankles.visual_focus: low armored shoe shells, black sole bands, ankle joint clearance

legs.visual_focus: slim thigh/shin armor, knee articulation reveals, calf taper

hips_torso_chest_back.visual_focus: tapered waist, armored chest contour, rear spine/hip fairings

arms.visual_focus: slim shoulder/upper-arm/forearm shells and elbow articulation gaps

neck_head_face.visual_focus: donor face alignment, hard-plastic head boundary, neck carrier fit

part_review.review_contract: Every generated bodykit part is assigned to a region for visual review, clearance review, panel-gap review, and manufacturing follow-up. The report is not a final aesthetic approval.

face_alignment_validation.reference_source: eliza_front_reference.png visible-face proportions, excluding hair and glasses

neck_carrier.role: black underbody carrier between R1 torso/head envelope and the donor-derived face

face_plate_details.role: visible hard-plastic face plate and seated detail inserts

hair_reference_only.role: hair is retained as silhouette/reference context only and is not generated as robot geometry

face_shell_depth_check.reason: Prevents stress-driven over-flattening of the donor-derived parametric face while 2D face proportions still pass.

face_production_surfacing.surface_method: fixed-yz-grid donor mesh sampling with closed x/y section lofts

hair_reference_policy.alignment_use: silhouette/context only; do not create hair mesh, ponytail mass, or collision volume

wrist_collision_policy.classification: controller keepout or wrist/forearm geometry issue, not a face-surfacing target
wrist_collision_policy.required_resolution: Use head-protection keepout, joint-limit policy, or wrist/forearm redesign for wrist-roll rows; do not flatten or shrink the donor-derived face solely to satisfy extreme wrist sweep contact.

face_alignment_validation.note: Face alignment uses the donor-derived parametric face shell plus separate eye/lip inserts. Hair and glasses in the concept references are reference-only and are not generated.

design_source_audit.note: This audit verifies source provenance only. It does not prove that primitive EVT geometry has already been rebuilt from OEM offset envelopes.

parametric_reconstruction_audit.completion_gate: blocked-until-simple shell primitives are rebuilt as fixed-grid loft/surface CAD with connector parameters

source_subassembly_anchor.connection_method: forearm-end collar with moving wrist roll kept clear

neck_carrier.name: neck_carrier
neck_carrier.panel_split_strategy: torso_link-mounted black neck carrier following R1 head_yaw/head_pitch and torso_collision envelopes

source_subassembly_anchor.anchor_role: black neck carrier between R1 torso and head envelope

rear_pelvis_bridge.name: rear_pelvis_bridge
rear_pelvis_bridge.panel_split_strategy: configured rear pelvis bridge anchored to pelvis_link

rear_torso_back_panel.name: rear_torso_back_panel
rear_torso_back_panel.panel_split_strategy: rear spine armor with separate black channel and side cut inserts

source_subassembly_anchor.anchor_role: rear torso back spine panel and black cut inserts

right_chest_side_panel.name: right_chest_side_panel
right_chest_side_panel.panel_split_strategy: right mirrored chest side armor panel with local black vent insert

right_foot_ankle.name: right_foot_ankle
right_foot_ankle.panel_split_strategy: right ankle-roll mounted boot upper, toe cap, outsole band, and rear heel block

source_subassembly_anchor.anchor_role: right ankle roll boot shell and outsole

right_forearm.name: right_forearm
right_forearm.panel_split_strategy: right forearm sleeve, blade, and inner detail mounted to elbow link before wrist-roll motion

source_subassembly_anchor.connection_method: offset shell to elbow-link OEM body envelope before wrist roll

right_glute_backside.name: right_glute_backside
right_glute_backside.panel_split_strategy: configured right glute/backside skin anchored to pelvis_link

right_hip_upper_leg.name: right_hip_upper_leg
right_hip_upper_leg.panel_split_strategy: right hip-yaw mounted thigh sleeve and armor only; pelvis rear fairings stay in rear_pelvis_glute

right_knee_shin.name: right_knee_shin
right_knee_shin.panel_split_strategy: right knee-link mounted shin sleeve plus front and side armor skins

source_subassembly_anchor.anchor_role: right knee-link shin sleeve and split armor skins
source_subassembly_anchor.connection_method: offset shell to knee/shin OEM body envelope

right_rear_hip_fairing.name: right_rear_hip_fairing
right_rear_hip_fairing.panel_split_strategy: configured right posterior hip fairing anchored to pelvis with right hip source context

right_shoulder.name: right_shoulder
right_shoulder.panel_split_strategy: right shoulder cap and fastener indexed to shoulder pitch/roll OEM anchors

source_subassembly_anchor.connection_method: source-body-mounted shoulder cosmetic shell

right_upper_arm.name: right_upper_arm
right_upper_arm.panel_split_strategy: right upper-arm sleeve and armor mounted to the shoulder-yaw source body

source_subassembly_anchor.connection_method: offset shell to shoulder-yaw OEM body envelope

right_wrist_cuff.name: right_wrist_cuff
right_wrist_cuff.panel_split_strategy: right slim cuff mounted to forearm end with right_wrist_roll_link reserved as a keepout, not a cosmetic mount

source_subassembly_anchor.connection_method: forearm-end collar with moving wrist roll kept clear

torso_chest_core_shell.name: torso_chest_core_shell
torso_chest_core_shell.panel_split_strategy: single torso-mounted cosmetic shell following torso_collision and waist_yaw anchors

waist_abdomen.name: waist_abdomen
waist_abdomen.panel_split_strategy: configured waist abdomen source assembly anchored to torso_link and waist yaw/roll OEM meshes

feet_ankles_worker.review_contract: Own these source-body-mounted subassemblies as connected replacement shells. Preserve source robot body anchors, STEP export, volume accounting, fit clearance, panel-gap gates, and mechanical-stress blocker evidence.

legs_knees_shins_worker.review_contract: Own these source-body-mounted subassemblies as connected replacement shells. Preserve source robot body anchors, STEP export, volume accounting, fit clearance, panel-gap gates, and mechanical-stress blocker evidence.

hips_pelvis_torso_worker.review_contract: Own these source-body-mounted subassemblies as connected replacement shells. Preserve source robot body anchors, STEP export, volume accounting, fit clearance, panel-gap gates, and mechanical-stress blocker evidence.

arms_shoulders_wrists_worker.review_contract: Own these source-body-mounted subassemblies as connected replacement shells. Preserve source robot body anchors, STEP export, volume accounting, fit clearance, panel-gap gates, and mechanical-stress blocker evidence.

neck_head_face_worker.review_contract: Own these source-body-mounted subassemblies as connected replacement shells. Preserve source robot body anchors, STEP export, volume accounting, fit clearance, panel-gap gates, and mechanical-stress blocker evidence.

subassembly_volume_report.note: Subassembly grouping is evidence that parts are mounted to source robot bodies and reference OEM meshes. Wrist cuffs are forearm-end collars with wrist-roll bodies reserved as moving keepouts, and configured hand subassemblies are source keepouts because this bodykit pass does not generate cosmetic hand shells. It is not final fastening, screw-boss, insert, or molded-rib design.
